entries.d: To arrive at a place
entries.s: How do you _____ the airport from here by public transport on a weekday morning?

entries.d: When a plane leaves the ground and goes up into the sky
entries.s: Our aeroplane to Spain will _____ from the runway in about thirty minutes at exactly ten o'clock.

entries.d: To board a vehicle like a bus or aircraft
entries.s: We need to quickly _____ the train at platform 3 before it leaves the station in two minutes.

entries.d: To travel through the air in a plane
entries.s: We will _____ back home to Vienna from Paris on Sunday evening at 7 pm.

entries.d: To step out of a vehicle like a bus or aircraft
entries.s: Please wait until the bus comes to a complete stop at the bus stop before you _____.

entries.d: To feel ill because you are very high up in the mountains
entries.s: Some climbers _____ very badly when they climbed too high and too fast up the Himalayan mountain.

entries.d: When a plane comes down and touches the ground
entries.s: The plane from London will _____ at the airport runway in about ten minutes and passengers will get off soon after.

entries.d: Used to say how much time is needed
entries.s: It _____ me about twenty minutes to walk to school from my house at a normal speed.

entries.d: To climb into a vehicle
entries.s: She opened the door and _____ the back seat of the car and put on her seatbelt for the journey.

entries.d: To pay money to use something for a short time
entries.s: My parents want to _____ a small red car for just one week during our summer holiday in Spain.

entries.d: To step out of a vehicle
entries.s: We _____ the parked car at the beach, grabbed our towels, and walked to the sand.

entries.d: To control and move a car
entries.s: My mum will _____ us home from the cinema in her blue car after the film finishes at 10 pm.

entries.d: To start a journey
entries.s: We need to _____ early in the morning at 6 am if we want to arrive at the airport on time.

entries.d: To spend time doing or creating something
entries.s: She is currently _____ on a big school history project about ancient Egypt for her homework assignment.

entries.d: To come near to something
entries.s: On this camping trip you can _____ close to nature and see wild birds, deer, and squirrels.

entries.d: To spend the night under canvas outdoors
entries.s: On our camping trip to the mountains, we _____ next to the cold lake under the stars for three nights.

entries.d: To get away from something unpleasant
entries.s: We sat in the cool shade under a big tree to _____ the burning hot afternoon sun from 12 to 3 pm.

entries.d: To go from one side to the other
entries.s: Be very careful when you _____ the busy main road near the school — look both ways first.

entries.d: To see friends at a planned time and location
entries.s: I'm going to _____ with my three best friends at the café after school today to celebrate the weekend.

entries.d: To start to be something
entries.s: She wants to _____ a children's doctor when she grows up and finishes her medical studies at university.

entries.d: Wanting to know or learn more about something
entries.s: The _____ little cat put its head into the open cardboard box to see what was inside.

entries.d: A choice that you make about something
entries.s: Moving to a new school in a different city was a very big _____ for our whole family last year.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with DECISION?

entries.d: Something that happens to you, or knowledge from doing things
entries.s: Visiting the Eiffel Tower in Paris was an amazing once-in-a-lifetime _____ for me — I will never forget it.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with EXPERIENCE?

entries.d: To travel around a new place to learn about it
entries.s: We spent the whole afternoon _____ the narrow winding streets of the old town and discovered a hidden church.

entries.d: A trip from one place to another
entries.s: The long _____ from Vienna to London by train takes about sixteen hours and crosses three countries.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with JOURNEY?

entries.d: Walking, not using a car or bus
entries.s: The bakery is very close — just 200 metres from here — let's go _____ instead of taking the car.

entries.d: Causing pain, hurting
entries.s: Falling off my bicycle and scraping my knee on the rough asphalt was very _____ — I cried for ten minutes.

entries.d: To arrive at a place or get to a point
entries.s: We finally _____ the top of the mountain after six hours of hiking, just before it got dark in the evening.

entries.d: To travel on water in a boat with sails
entries.s: They _____ across the large lake on their small wooden boat using the wind in the white sails.

entries.d: A person who goes on trips to different places
entries.s: The experienced _____ visited ten different countries around the world in just one year with her backpack.

entries.d: Feeling sad because you are alone
entries.s: She felt very _____ because she was all alone in the classroom during break and didn't know anyone yet.

entries.d: To say what is wrong with someone or something
entries.s: The strict teacher _____ my essay sharply because it was too short and had many spelling mistakes.

entries.d: A person who travels to new places to find out about them
entries.s: The famous _____ wrote a thrilling book about her discoveries in the African jungle where no one had been before.

entries.d: Although, despite the fact that
entries.s: _____ it was raining heavily outside, we still decided to go for a walk in the park with umbrellas.

entries.d: A small, simple house or building
entries.s: The old poor fisherman lived in a tiny wooden _____ with just one room near the sandy sea beach.

entries.d: To be discovered to be true in the end
entries.s: The big scary maths test _____ to be much easier than I had thought — I got a good mark!

entries.d: A wild area where no people live
entries.s: There are wild brown bears, grey wolves, and other dangerous animals in the remote _____ far from any town.

entries.d: To act in a certain way
entries.s: Please _____ yourselves well and quietly when we visit the museum — don't run around or shout.

entries.d: Thinking about everything together
entries.s: _____, it was a wonderful summer holiday with sunshine, swimming, and lots of ice cream.

entries.d: Not sleeping
entries.s: The noisy baby was still _____ crying at midnight and her mother was getting tired.

entries.d: Quite, fairly (not about looks here)
entries.s: The history homework was _____ easy — not super simple, but not really hard either, somewhere in the middle.

entries.d: Used to say you are sorry about something
entries.s: _____, the big rock concert is completely sold out and we cannot buy any more tickets for Saturday night.

entries.d: The time when a plane, bus or train leaves
entries.s: Check the big screen in the airport terminal for the exact _____ time of your flight to Rome.

entries.d: A trip in a plane from one place to another
entries.s: Our _____ to sunny Rome leaves from Vienna airport gate 12 at exactly half past eight tomorrow morning.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with FLIGHT?
I don't want to _____ my flight!

entries.d: To book a table, room or seat in advance
entries.s: I'd like to _____ for a table for two people at exactly seven o'clock this evening, please.

entries.d: To notice or pay attention to something
entries.s: Please _____ carefully on your calendar that the school library closes at exactly five o'clock today.

entries.d: To repair something that is broken
entries.s: My dad is very good with tools — he can _____ almost anything that breaks in the house, from taps to TVs.

entries.d: Wanting or needing a drink
entries.s: After the long, hot football match in the sunshine, I was extremely _____ and drank two glasses of water.

entries.d: Not able to happen or be done
entries.s: It's completely _____ to finish all 100 pages of this long homework assignment in just one single hour.

entries.d: Not long ago, a short time in the past
entries.s: I _____ started learning how to play the drums — just three weeks ago actually — and I love it so far.

entries.d: To not know where you are
entries.s: We _____ completely in the big unfamiliar forest because we didn't have a map or a compass.
mc: to know exactly where we were

entries.d: To learn more about a person or place over time
entries.s: I want to _____ my new classmates better by spending time with them during break and after school.

entries.d: To say that you will definitely do something
entries.s: I _____ to definitely help you with your maths homework every day after school this week — you can count on me.

entries.d: To say that something is good and others should try it
entries.s: I can really _____ this new pizza restaurant to everyone — the food is great and the prices are low!
